title: Ceropegia cedrimontana
taxonomy: Ceropegia cedrimontana is classified within the family Apocynaceae, commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. This family is part of the larger order Gentianales, which includes other flowering plants such as the gentians and the willows. The order Gentianales is further grouped into the clade Asterids, a diverse group of flowering plants. Within the Asterids, the Apocynaceae family is part of the larger clade Lamiales, which includes plants with five-lobed corollas. The classification of Ceropegia cedrimontana reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants within the Asterids.

intro: Ceropegia cedrimontana, also known as the "string of hearts," is a flowering succulent plant native to the Western Ghats region of India. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Ceropegia cedrimontana include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which have been identified as the primary compounds responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Ceropegia cedrimontana is often used to prepare herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which are used to treat a range of health conditions.
